purpose: Generate a production-quality Python agent for a fictional SaaS company that can classify intent, answer pricing/features questions from a local knowledge base, retain multi-turn state, and only trigger lead capture after collecting all required lead fields.
primary_goal: Build a clean, demo-ready, real-world deployable conversational AI agent using LangGraph (preferred) or AutoGen, with local RAG, controlled tool execution, and testable memory behavior.

stack_requirements.framework: LangGraph preferred; LangChain acceptable if needed
stack_requirements.memory_layer: Use SimpleMem for persistent conversational memory / retrieval support, while keeping the product knowledge base local and deterministic for factual answers.

hard_constraints: Do not use web search at runtime.
Do not hardcode dynamic answers outside the local knowledge base.
Do not trigger lead capture before all required fields are collected.
Do not ask for unnecessary personal data.
Do not lose conversation state across at least 5-6 turns.
Do not answer pricing/policy questions from model memory when a local KB lookup is available.

business_context.product: Automated video editing SaaS for content creators

items: 24/7 support available only on Pro plan

expected_intents.label: casual_greeting
expected_intents.definition: Greetings, small talk, or non-buying conversation.

expected_intents.label: product_or_pricing_inquiry
expected_intents.definition: Questions about pricing, plans, features, policies, limits, or comparisons.

expected_intents.label: high_intent_lead
expected_intents.definition: User signals readiness to buy, try, upgrade, book, sign up, or start immediately.

tooling.lead_capture_function: def mock_lead_capture(name, email, platform): print(f"Lead captured successfully: {name}, {email}, {platform}")
tooling.tool_rule: Only call mock_lead_capture after all three values are collected: name, email, platform.

simplemem_role: preserve prior user preferences within the session
support retrieval of recent context across 5-6 turns
memory_strategy.simplemem_usage_note: Use SimpleMem as the durable memory layer and session context manager, but keep product facts in the local knowledge base so answers remain deterministic.

performance_targets.intent_classification_accuracy: >= 95% on a small synthetic test set
performance_targets.rag_factual_accuracy: 100% exact on all local KB questions
performance_targets.lead_slot_completion: 100% once the user provides all three fields
performance_targets.memory_retention: Must preserve state correctly across at least 6 turns
performance_targets.hallucination_rate: As close to 0 as possible for KB-backed facts
response_behavior: ask one concise follow-up question at a time
summarize current collected lead fields before requesting the next missing one
never call the tool when fields are incomplete

files_to_create.purpose: Main agent graph, routing, response generation, and tool gating.
Intent classifier with deterministic rules plus optional LLM fallback.
Local KB loader, retrieval, and answer synthesis.
SimpleMem wrapper for conversation memory and state restoration.
Mock lead capture tool and validation helpers.
Local product facts, pricing, and policies.
Ensure the lead capture tool never runs early.
Check exact answers for KB facts.
Step-by-step build workflow for Cursor execution.

cursor_execution_mode.rule: Implement one phase at a time. After each phase, produce runnable code and preserve prior working state.

iteration_plan.name: Project scaffold and data design
definition_of_done: KB file contains exact pricing/features/policy facts

iteration_plan.name: Intent classifier
definition_of_done: All three intents are classified reliably
greeting, inquiry, and high-intent examples are covered

iteration_plan.name: Local RAG layer
definition_of_done: Pricing and policy questions are answered only from the KB

iteration_plan.name: SimpleMem integration
definition_of_done: Lead fields persist until tool execution
Memory can be inspected or logged

iteration_plan.name: Lead qualification and tool gating
definition_of_done: Agent asks for name, email, platform only when high intent is detected
Tool runs only after all three fields are present

iteration_plan.name: README and demo readiness
definition_of_done: A new user can run the project locally
WhatsApp integration is described at a practical level

general: Build for clarity first, then polish.
Prefer deterministic logic for intent and lead gating.
Use the LLM only where natural language generation or soft classification helps.
Keep answers brief, accurate, and sales-ready.
Log state transitions clearly for debugging.
response_policy: For greetings: respond warmly and ask how you can help.
For product/pricing inquiries: retrieve exact KB facts and answer directly.
For high intent: acknowledge interest and start slot collection.
For incomplete lead data: ask only for the missing field(s).
For complete lead data: call mock_lead_capture exactly once.
quality_rules: Prefer simple, readable code over clever code.
Include validation for email and platform values.
Keep knowledge base isolated from logic.

functional: Agent answers pricing correctly from local KB.
Agent detects high intent from phrases like 'I want to try', 'sign me up', 'start Pro plan'.
Agent collects name, email, and platform before tool call.
Agent keeps state across multiple turns.
technical: Project runs locally with documented steps.
Tests verify intent, RAG, and tool gating.
README includes architecture and WhatsApp webhook integration explanation.
demo_ready: Can show pricing Q&A in one flow.
Can show successful mock lead capture.

sections: Definition of done for each phase

final_output_expectation: A complete repository that can be cloned and run locally, with a believable conversational flow, deterministic knowledge answers, safe tool execution, and polished documentation.
